item.title: ⚔️ US Launches Retaliatory Strikes on Iran After Apache Helicopter Downed
item.description: The US military carried out strikes on Iran after an Iranian Shahed drone shot down a US Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. Both pilots were rescued safely. Iran vowed to respond to what it called unjustified aggression.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🚁 Iranian Armed Drone Shot Down US Apache Helicopter, Sources Confirm
item.description: An Iranian Shahed drone struck the US Apache helicopter on patrol over the Strait of Hormuz. The two pilots were rescued within two hours. Shahed drones have been used extensively by Iran and supplied to Russia for the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: CBS News

item.title: 🏛️ Congress Backs Iran War Powers Resolutions for First Time
item.description: For the first time since the Iran war began, the Republican-led Congress has backed resolutions that could block Trump from continuing hostilities, reflecting growing concern within his party about the three-month conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 Congress Sends $70 Billion Immigration Enforcement Bill to Trump
item.description: House Republicans passed a massive $70 billion funding package for ICE and Border Patrol through 2029, ending a political stalemate and sending the bill to President Trumps desk.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Considers Stricter AI Chip Export Controls to Align With US
item.description: Taiwan authorities are considering tighter export controls on AI chip sales to China to further align with US restrictions, amid growing technology competition between Washington and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Japan Times

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Japan to Name China as Biggest Defense Concern in New Document
item.description: Japan Defense Ministry expected to designate China as the most serious concern in its annual defense document, reflecting growing regional tensions.
item.dc:creator: Nikkei Asia

item.title: 🇮🇷⚛️ Iran to Dilute Uranium Stockpile Rather Than Hand Over in US Deal
item.description: Iran will dilute its uranium stockpile rather than hand it over as part of negotiations with the US, while talks focus on four key issues including halting Iranian uranium enrichment.
item.dc:creator: Times of Israel

item.title: 🇺🇸⚠️ Violent Threats Against US Lawmakers Spiked After Meta Eased Moderation
item.description: A watchdog report found explicit calls for murder and sexual harassment targeting US lawmakers on Facebook increased after Meta relaxed content moderation policies.
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: 💥⚠️ Commentary: Middle East Hostilities a Question of When, Not If
item.description: Analysis suggests that despite temporary pauses in fighting between Israel and Iran, renewed hostilities remain likely as both sides leave the door open for escalation.
item.dc:creator: Channel News Asia

item.title: 🇷🇺⛽ Fuel Supply Disruptions Trigger Panic-Buying in Southern Russia
item.description: Disruptions to fuel supplies triggered panic-buying in Russias Krasnodar region, the governor said, as Ukrainian strikes on energy infrastructure hit fuel deliveries across several southern regions and Russian-held Crimea.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US House Passes $70 Billion Immigration Enforcement Package
item.description: The Republican-controlled House passed a $70 billion bill to fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol through the remainder of President Trumps administration, ending a longstanding political stalemate over immigration funding.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇦 Saskatchewan Marshals Service Poaching Officers from RCMP and Municipal Police Forces
item.description: New provincial marshal service accused of draining talent from existing RCMP and municipal police as it builds its ranks, with 25 of 27 officers reportedly recruited from other forces.
item.dc:creator: CBC

item.title: 🇮🇳 Indias Opposition Demands Government Absorb Oil Price Shock to Protect Growth
item.description: Congress party urges government to shield consumers from rising fuel costs rather than passing them on, warning that higher prices would hurt demand and slow economic growth.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canadas Mark Carney Champions Alliance of Mid-Level Powers to Counter US Protectionism
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carneys push for a coalition of mid-sized economies faces the reality of deep North American trade dependence as Trumps protectionism reshapes global commerce.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🛢️ Global Oil Inventories Headed Toward Multi-Decade Lows as Iran War Drains Stockpiles
item.description: US Energy Information Administration warns that oil stockpiles in major economies are on track for their lowest levels since 2003 as Iran conflict cuts output at record pace.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russian Military Official Killed in Car Bombing Near Moscow
item.description: Damir Davydov, a Russian official responsible for missile and artillery ammunition supplies, was killed in a car explosion outside Moscow. The incident occurred in a residential area for military personnel.
item.dc:creator: Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇫🇷⛪ France Condemns Attacks on Christians in East Jerusalem
item.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot denounced what he described as a growing wave of violence against Christians in East Jerusalem and Israel, calling for protection of religious sites.
item.dc:creator: Daily Sabah

item.title: 🇯🇵🔌 Japan Seeks Bigger Role in Asia Subsea Cable Networks as AI Rewires Demand
item.description: Japan is positioning itself to play a larger role in Asias subsea cable infrastructure as AI drives new demand patterns for data connectivity across the region.
item.dc:creator: Nikkei Asia

item.title: 🇺🇸⚽ White House Defends World Cup Visa Bans for Iranian Officials and Somali Referee
item.description: The head of the White House World Cup Task Force defended decisions to deny visas to certain Iranian officials and a Somali referee ahead of the 2026 tournament, citing security reasons.
item.dc:creator: Al-Monitor

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Announces Fresh Sanctions Package Banning Russian Soldiers From Entering Bloc
item.description: The European Union unveiled its 21st sanctions package targeting Moscow, including a ban on Russian soldiers entering the bloc and new measures against individuals and entities supporting Russia's war effort.
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: 🚜⛽ US Farmers Hit by Record Diesel Prices as Iran War Chokes Fuel Supplies
item.description: High energy costs are squeezing grain and soybean growers across the US farm belt as the Iran war chokes fuel supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, pushing diesel prices to record highs in key agricultural states.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇧💥 Israel Strikes Tyre, Killing 8, Then Orders Evacuation of Entire Lebanese City
item.description: Israel struck the historic port city of Tyre in southern Lebanon killing at least 8 people, minutes later ordering the evacuation of the entire city for the first time in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ☀️ Hanwha Qcells launches first fully onshore US solar supply chain
item.description: South Korean company begins solar cell production in the US, establishing the country’s first integrated supply chain amid push for clean energy independence.
item.dc:creator: Nikkei Asia

item.title: 🇵🇸 UN report documents systematic rights violations against Palestinians
item.description: UN inquiry finds civilians in Gaza and West Bank subjected to severe rights violations by Israeli forces and settlers, as tensions escalate in the region.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark fails to disclose soaring F-35 costs, auditors say
item.description: Danish State Auditors criticize Defense Ministry for not properly informing lawmakers about the true price of one of the country's largest defense purchases.
item.dc:creator: Anadolu Agency
